articles.title: BREAKING: Telegram To Be Banned In India Now? Delhi High Court To Hear Plea! | Trending News |News18
articles.description: A petition seeking a ban on Telegram in India is set to be heard by the Delhi High Court, sparking a major debate over digital platforms, online safety, and regulatory oversight. The plea raises concerns regarding the platform's alleged misuse and calls for stricter action by authorities. The case could have significant implications for millions of Telegram users across India, as the court examines the legal and policy issues involved. articles.description: The TMC dispute turns on a question the Tenth Schedule of the Indian Constitution has never fully settled — does a two-thirds split within Parliament amount to a merger, or must the larger political party itself merge first?

The suggestion has sparked a heated online discussion, with some agreeing and seeking advice, while others criticize the idea, citing the benefits of staying in India.

articles.title: Trump's Obama Rant Takes Wild Turn as He Drops Profanity During Iran Deal Remarks | WATCH
articles.description: President Donald Trump sparked fresh controversy at the G7 Summit in France after dropping a profanity while criticizing Barack Obama's Iran nuclear deal. During remarks about his proposed agreement with Tehran, Trump claimed Iran mocked Obama and defended his own approach to negotiations. The expletive-laced moment quickly went viral, overshadowing ongoing discussions surrounding the new U.S.-Iran deal and a reported $300 billion reconstruction proposal.

articles.title: ‘People thought I was crazy’: Former Delhi Police officer quits government job and starts over in Australia

He successfully built a new life from scratch, becoming a homeowner and a real estate agent in Adelaide, emphasizing that his journey was driven by taking chances and perseverance, not luck.

articles.title: Breaking: US-Iran MoU signed remotely by both Trump and Pezeshkian to end the brutal Middle East war
articles.description: A historic diplomatic breakthrough appears to have taken effect as the United States and Iran reportedly signed a memorandum of understanding aimed at ending months of conflict and opening a new phase of negotiations. According to multiple reports, U.S. President Donald Trump and Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian signed the agreement remotely, bringing the framework into force ahead of a planned diplomatic meeting. The deal is expected to focus on ending hostilities, restoring regional stability, reopening key shipping routes and launching further talks on Iran’s nuclear program, sanctions relief and broader security issues.

Mike Collins in Georgia’s high-stakes Senate race. Trump also promised to hold major rallies in the state, escalating the political battle ahead of the election.The remarks come as Republicans look to unseat Ossoff in one of the most closely watched contests of the 2026 cycle. Watch the latest developments, reactions, and what Trump’s endorsement could mean for the Georgia showdown.

articles.title: 'Lakhs of Indians working as seafarers': PM Modi raises maritime safety in meeting with Trump
articles.description: Prime Minister Modi and US President Trump met at the G7 Summit, prioritizing maritime security and the safety of Indian seafarers. Modi stressed the vital importance of open shipping routes, particularly the Strait of Hormuz, and commended Trump's efforts in West Asia. The leaders also discussed trade deals, signaling renewed energy in India-US relations after a period of strain.
